thread.title: Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi announced funds of 50 crores for the National Police Academy

title: Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi announced funds of 50 crores for the National Police Academy
text:
(24News) Federal Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi announced Rs 50 crore funds‚Ā§ for the‚Äć National Police Academy .
Addressing the passing-out‚ÄĆ parade at the National Police Academy Islamabad, Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi said, ‚ÄúHappy day to all ASPs‚Ā£ and policemen who have passed out. Your hard work is coming to an end today. If you are successful in serving the public, then you are successful in ‚ĀĘeverything. If you police officers are not‚ÄĆ doing anything for the public, then it ‚ÄĆis ‚Ā£of no use to us.
The Interior‚Ā§ Minister‚ÄĆ said that the National Police Academy‚ÄĆ is a good institution, the ‚ĀĘNational Police Academy has to be made an international ‚ĀĘstandard academy, the National Police Academy still‚Ā£ lacks‚ÄĆ many things, we have formed a task force for‚Ā£ this work. ‚ÄčNext year‚Ā§ it should be on the style of Pakistan Military Academy, officers should not be posted here ‚Ā§as punishment,‚Ā£ like the army,‚Ā§ the best officers should be posted here.
Mohsin‚ĀĘ Naqvi said that it is‚Äč desired that the allowances‚ĀĘ of the officers posted ‚Ā£here‚Äč are also better, this academy will be made into a PMA-style academy in one year, I have ‚ĀĘcome here today with an arrangement of 50 crores, we ‚Äćwill immediately ‚ĀĘgive 50 ‚ÄĆcrores for this academy. Will invest Rs.
The interior minister ‚ÄĆsaid that the elite‚Ā£ training center in Lahore‚Ā£ is also the best center in ‚Ā£Pakistan, we have ‚Äćto train the officers here instead ‚Äćof sending the police officers‚Ā§ here for training elsewhere, we have to bring the best officers to the National Police‚ĀĘ Academy. IGs of every field are present here, the first priority is ‚Ā£your ‚ĀĘprovince.
Mohsin Naqvi said‚ĀĘ that keep‚Ā§ your attachment with this academy, these 10 officers have to‚Äć make many decisions on a daily‚ÄĆ basis, we will also ‚Ā£start ‚Äčwork ‚Ā£on short courses for officers soon, the officers who are graduating today will again Congratulations, may Allah ‚Äćbless the‚ÄĆ passing out officers and police men.
What are the ‚ĀĘpotential benefits of increased funding for the National Police Academy ‚ĀĘas discussed by Dr.‚ĀĘ Amina ‚Ā§Farooq? Interview‚ÄĆ Between Time.news Editor and Security Expert Dr. Amina Farooq
Time.news‚Äč Editor (TNE): Good day, Dr. Farooq. Thank ‚Äčyou for joining us to discuss ‚Äćthe recent announcement ‚Ā§made by‚Ā§ Federal Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi regarding the Rs‚Ā§ 50 crore ‚Äćfunds for the National Police Academy.
Dr. Amina‚Äć Farooq ‚Ā§(AF): Thank you for having me. It‚Äôs a ‚Äćpleasure to discuss this important development.
TNE: Minister Naqvi highlighted this ‚Äčfunding as‚Äć a ‚Ā§means to support the ‚Äčnewly minted Assistant Superintendents of Police (ASPs) and policemen. In‚ĀĘ your view, what impact could ‚Ā§this funding ‚ĀĘhave on the National Police‚ÄĆ Academy and the police force as a whole?
AF: The Rs ‚ĀĘ50 crore allocation is significant. It signals a commitment to modernizing our‚Ā§ police force‚Äč and ‚Ā£enhancing training facilities. This funding could lead to improvements‚ĀĘ in educational resources,‚Ā§ updated technology for training, and possibly even better living‚ÄĆ conditions‚ÄĆ for the cadets. Ultimately, well-trained police officers are better equipped‚ĀĘ to serve the public.
TNE: Do you think the quality of ‚Ā§training at the academy has been‚Ā§ a concern previously? What ‚Äčchanges would be most effective?
AF: Indeed,‚Äč there have been longstanding‚Äć concerns about the adequacy of training within our police ‚Ā§forces. Focusing on community policing, ethics in law‚Äč enforcement, ‚Äčand modern investigative techniques could greatly enhance the effectiveness of the police. Moreover, investing in mental health resources for officers would also be crucial, as policing ‚ĀĘcan be a highly stressful profession.
TNE: Minister Naqvi mentioned‚Äč that success in serving‚ĀĘ the‚Ā£ public equals success in their careers. How‚Ā§ can the police improve their relationship with the communities they serve?
AF: Building trust‚Ā§ is paramount. Continuous ‚Ā§community engagement initiatives, open forums for dialog, ‚Äćand transparency in operations will ‚ÄĆhelp foster a better‚ĀĘ relationship. Additionally, ‚Ā£training ‚Äčpolice ‚ÄĆofficers in cultural competency‚Ā£ can ‚Ā£ensure ‚ĀĘthey‚Ā£ understand and respect the diverse communities within their jurisdiction.
TNE: Speaking of training, ‚Ā£do you think ‚Ā£the current curriculum ‚Ā£at the‚Ā£ National Police Academy is adequate for ‚ÄĆpreparing ‚ĀĘofficers to face modern challenges?
AF: ‚Ā§ The curriculum is evolving, but there still seems to be a gap in addressing contemporary issues‚Äć like cybercrime, terrorism, and social media‚Äôs role in ‚ĀĘpolicing. ‚Ā§The introduction of specialized modules targeting these ‚Ā§areas would be beneficial. Continuous education‚Äć programs after graduation should also be ‚Äča ‚ĀĘpriority.
TNE: ‚ÄĆ With the new funding, what innovations or ‚Äćtechnologies would you like to see implemented in the ‚Äčtraining process?
AF: I‚Äôd advocate for incorporating simulations that reflect real-life scenarios using virtual‚Äč reality, which can provide immersive training‚ÄĆ experiences‚ĀĘ without the risks. Additionally, adopting data analysis tools can help future officers understand‚ÄĆ crime ‚Äćtrends ‚Ā§and devise data-driven strategies for crime prevention.
TNE: Lastly, what do you‚Äć think the ‚Äčlong-term implications of this ‚Äčfunding ‚ÄĆcould be‚Äć on the country‚Äôs security ‚Ā§as a whole?
AF: If the funds are ‚Ā§appropriately allocated and utilized, we could see a ‚Äćgradual transformation ‚Ā§in our‚Äć policing standards. Enhanced‚Ā§ training ‚Ā£can lead to a more professional police force, which in turn could reduce crime rates‚ĀĘ and improve overall public safety. More importantly, it would establish a foundation of trust and accountability that is ‚Äćessential for a thriving democratic society.
TNE: Thank‚Äč you, Dr. Farooq, for‚Ā§ your insights. ‚ÄćThis certainly ‚ĀĘsheds light on the broader implications of the funding for the National Police Academy ‚Äćand the future of policing in‚Äč our country.
AF: Thank ‚Äćyou for the opportunity to discuss this crucial topic!
